name: Arabian Paganism
note: The pre-Islamic religion of the Arabian peninsula; centered on tribal gods, sacred stones (especially the Black Stone at the Kaaba in Mecca), and astral deities. The Nabataean pantheon (Dushara, al-Uzza, Allat, Manat) was an Arabian pagan tradition blending with Hellenistic influences. Islam explicitly targeted and replaced this tradition in the 7th century.
